spec_match.rationale: The visible popover uses popover="auto" and showPopover(), and the visible focus demo uses a native <dialog>.showModal() inside the :focus-within container.

interactive.rationale: DevTools MCP exercised the native popover, the popover :active test button, the native modal dialog, and the dialog input; each updated the visible log/readout.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page now matches its Chrome 149 top-layer boundary copy by testing real popover and dialog top-layer elements instead of custom div overlays.

edge_cases.rationale: The demo surfaces :hover, :active, and :focus-within boundary checks, including a clear Chrome 148 pre-target leak state for the v149 focus-within assertion.

references.rationale: The concept page now directly links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/6296574159355904 and the Selectors Level 4 user action pseudo-class reference.

design_system.rationale: The native popover/dialog UI uses existing CSS variables, button color/background pairs, explicit button types, and labelled modal dialog controls.

summary: Pre-fix DevTools MCP confirmed #info-popover was a plain DIV with no popover attribute and #focus-dialog was a plain DIV, while the hidden probe used a real dialog. Post-fix Chrome 148 testing at http://localhost:3000/v149/user-action-pseudo-class-top-layer-boundary/interaction-scope-demo/ confirmed the visible popover opens with showPopover() and :popover-open, the popover :active test leaves the card inactive, the visible focus demo opens a real modal DIALOG with showModal(), and the dialog input reports the current browser's pre-v149 :focus-within leak clearly. Console and network were clean. The feature conformance route reported 6 PASS, 1 FAIL in Chrome 148, with the failing focus-within boundary assertion matching the page's visible pre-target leak state for this Chrome 149 feature.
